name: Cloudinary API
what_it_does: Cloud-based image and video management platform with on-the-fly transformations, AI-powered analysis, global CDN delivery, and APIs for programmatic media upload and management.
use_cases: Uploading and transforming images programmatically from agent workflows
Generating resized/cropped/optimized image variants via URL parameters
AI-powered image analysis (face detection, content moderation) from agents
Managing media asset libraries with metadata and tagging
not_for: Teams needing full self-hosted media processing
Simple static file hosting without transformation needs
best_when: An agent needs to upload, transform, and serve images/videos at scale with minimal infrastructure management.
avoid_when: You only need file storage without image transformations, or need fully self-hosted media processing.

auth.notes: API key + API secret for signed operations. Cloud name identifies the account. Unsigned uploads allowed for public assets. HMAC-SHA256 signature for sensitive operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 25 monthly credits (1 credit = 1k transformations or 1GB storage or 1GB bandwidth)
pricing.notes: Credit-based pricing unifies storage, transformations, and bandwidth. Free tier is usable for development. Credits can be supplemented with add-ons.

performance.rate_limits: Admin API: 500 requests/hour; Upload API: 500 requests/second burst

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear error responses with error.message field. Upload API errors include HTTP status codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Upload with public_id is effectively idempotent - re-uploading same public_id replaces the asset.
known_agent_gotchas: Admin API has strict rate limits (500/hour) - agents doing bulk operations will hit limits
Signed URLs require timestamp to be within 1 hour - agent clock skew causes failures
Transformation credits consumed at runtime - unexpected transformations inflate costs
Eager transformations are asynchronous - agents must poll or use webhooks for completion
Default image quality is not always optimal for web - agents should specify quality parameter
